name: RETROPERITONEAL ONCOCYTOMA
definition: ONCOCYTIC ADRENOCORTICAL ADENOMA / NEOPLASM PROXIMAL TUBULAR ADENOMA = BENIGN OXYPHILIC ADENOMA rare form of a benign hypervascular adenoma

demographics.Histo: abundant deeply eosinophilic (mitochondria-rich) cells; positive for vimentin, melan
demographics.Path: well-encapsulated tan-colored tumor of well-differentiated proximal tubular cells +

dominant_finding: hypointense relative to renal parenchyma on T1WI (70%)
discriminating_features: well-marginated often large mass with heterogeneous enhancement of nonspecific
renal mass of homogeneously low attenuation / hypoechogenicity (in > 50%)
central stellate scar in 30% (in lesions > 3 cm in diameter ← organization of central

general: well-marginated often large mass with heterogeneous enhancement of nonspecific
renal mass of homogeneously low attenuation / hypoechogenicity (in > 50%)
central stellate scar in 30% (in lesions > 3 cm in diameter ← organization of central
invasion of renal capsule / renal vein in large tumors
MR: hypointense relative to renal parenchyma on T1WI (70%)
hyperintense relative to renal parenchyma on T2WI (67%)
hypointense on T1WI + hyperintense on T2WI
less enhancement than remainder of mass
ANGIO: spoke-wheel configuration in 80% (DDx: renal cell ca.)
NO contrast puddling / arteriovenous shunting / renal vein invasion
NUC: photopenic area on 99mTc-DMSA ← tubular cells do not function normally
